message:
feat(ipc): add RPC handler framework, notification config validation, and IPC client enhancements

Extends the IPC subsystem with a general-purpose RPC handler framework,
validates notification configuration semantics, and enhances the IPC
client with token and RPC support.

config.rs (152 insertions):
- Add NotificationConfig::validate() checking min_severity values,
  non-empty include/exclude patterns, valid agent_types, positive
  max_batch_size, and include/exclude overlap detection
- Add parse_notification_severity() and parse_notification_agent_type()
  helper functions for semantic validation
- Wire notification validation into Config::validate()

ipc.rs (450+ insertions):
- Add IpcRpcRequest struct for structured RPC calls with args and
  request_id fields
- Add IpcRpcHandler type alias (Arc<dyn Fn + Send + Sync>) for
  pluggable RPC command dispatch
- Add rpc_handler field to IpcHandlerContext
- Add IpcHandlerContext::with_auth_and_rpc() constructor
- Add IpcServer::run_with_registry_auth_and_rpc() combining all
  server capabilities
- Add handle_request_with_context() routing requests through auth
  then to appropriate handler (UserVar, Ping, Status, PaneState, Rpc)
- Add rpc_required_scope() mapping RPC args to required IPC scopes
- Add elapsed_ms() timing helper for request duration tracking
- Add IpcResponse::error_with_code() and with_timing() builder methods
  for structured error responses with timing metadata
- Extend IpcClient with set_token(), call_rpc(), and
  send_request_with_id() for authenticated RPC calls
- Add unit test for IpcResponse error_with_code serialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
